analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 9 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Unauthenticated JTAG/DMI path can drive debug control state. dm_csrs hardwires dmstatus.authenticated=1 and accepts DMI writes to DMControl that update haltreq/resumereq/dmactive/ndmreset-related state without any observed pass_chk/jtag_key authorization in dm_csrs/dm_top. csr_regfile enters debug mode directly on debug_req_i.
findings.vulnerability_category: DMI write selecting debug control registers to halt/resume or enter debug mode / hart debug entry/control state: haltreq_o, resumereq_o, debug_req_o, debug_mode_q, dpc/dscratch debug CSRs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debug requester via JTAG TAP tck/tms/td_i or SimJTAG/SimDTM Operation: DMI write selecting debug control registers to halt/resume or enter debug mode Object: hart debug entry/control state: haltreq_o, resumereq_o, debug_req_o, debug_mode_q, dpc/dscratch debug CSRs Expected guard: DMI/JTAG halt/resume/debug-entry operations should be gated by explicit debug authentication/authorization such as pass_chk/jtag_key validation and/or lifecycle/test authorization before asserting debug_req_o/haltreq_o/resumereq_o. Observed behavior: dm_csrs reports no authentication implemented and sets dmstatus.authenticated=1'b1. It accepts any ready/valid DMI write to DMControl and assigns dmcontrol_d=dmi_req_i.data. dm_top connects dm_csrs haltreq/resumereq to dm_mem, whose debug_req_o reaches the core. csr_regfile enters debug mode when debug_req_i && commit_instr_i[0].valid, without local auth check. Path: JTAG TAP -> dmi_jtag -> dmi_cdc -> dm_top/dm_csrs -> haltreq/resumereq -> dm_mem debug_req_o -> csr_regfile debug_req_i/debug_mode_q Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External JTAG/DMI requester can halt/resume harts or force debug entry without a proven authentication gate, exposing or modifying debug state.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full dmi_jtag state machine around pass_chk was truncated; available downstream evidence shows dm_csrs/dm_top do not consume pass_chk/jtag_key and advertise authenticated unconditionally.

findings.summary: Debug SBA register writes can create system-bus access requests without observed debug authentication or address/range policy checks at the CSR-to-SBA boundary.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write system bus via debug SBA registers SBAddress/SBData/SBCS / AXI-addressed system memory and MMIO reachable through dm_sba axi_m_req_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: DMI/JTAG requester acting through debug module system bus master Operation: read/write system bus via debug SBA registers SBAddress/SBData/SBCS Object: AXI-addressed system memory and MMIO reachable through dm_sba axi_m_req_o Expected guard: Debug SBA should require authenticated debug authorization and constrain addresses/ranges/access policy before issuing reads/writes to protected memory/MMIO. Observed behavior: dm_csrs exposes sbaddress_o/sbdata_o and asserts sbaddress_write_valid_o or sbdata_write_valid_o on DMI writes when sbcs_q.sberror==0. Busy sets sbbusyerror but the write/update block still executes due to a separate begin after the busy if. dm_top directly connects these signals to dm_sba, which drives axi_m_req_o. No visible auth/range guard is present in these excerpts. Path: DMI/JTAG -> dm_csrs SBCS/SBAddress/SBData registers -> dm_top i_dm_sba -> AXI master interface axi_m_req_o -> SoC memory/peripherals Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthenticated debug requester may read or write arbitrary AXI-reachable protected memory/MMIO via the debug master if dm_sba/fabric do not enforce ranges.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dm_sba implementation and fabric address policy were not provided; finding is based on visible CSR-to-SBA issuance path lacking auth/range mediation.

findings.summary: Abstract command/data register accesses are accepted from DMI based on ready/valid and cmdbusy only, with no observed authentication or halted/available target mediation before cmd_valid_o/data_o updates.
findings.vulnerability_category: execute abstract command or read/write debug data/progbuf registers / selected hart architectural/debug state reachable through cmd_valid_o, command_o, data_o/data_i, cmderror, halted/resumeack status / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external DMI requester Operation: execute abstract command or read/write debug data/progbuf registers Object: selected hart architectural/debug state reachable through cmd_valid_o, command_o, data_o/data_i, cmderror, halted/resumeack status Expected guard: Abstract commands and data register accesses should require authenticated active debug session, valid/available selected hart, halted/ready target, and busy/error mediation. Observed behavior: dm_csrs sets dmstatus.authenticated=1. DMI writes to Command assert cmd_valid_o when !cmdbusy_i and copy dmi_req_i.data to command_d. Data and progbuf writes similarly update storage and can trigger autoexec cmd_valid_o when !cmdbusy_i. Reads expose data_q and status for selected_hart. No auth or halted/unavailable gate is visible on command/data issuance. Path: DMI/JTAG -> dm_csrs DMI case decode for Data/Command/AbstractAuto/ProgBuf -> cmd_valid_o/data_o/progbuf_o -> dm_top dm_mem -> hart/debug memory interface Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: External DMI requester can initiate abstract commands or expose/modify debug data for a selected hart without a proven authentication guard; if downstream dm_mem does not block, hart register/debug state is at risk.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Downstream dm_mem abstract command implementation was not included; exact hart-state effects are inferred from command/data interface connectivity.

findings.summary: Privileged and debug CSR read/write decode lacks visible privilege-level gating; debug CSRs and machine/supervisor CSRs are read and written under csr_we/csr_read without checks against current privilege or debug_mode_q in the observed cases.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write privileged CSR / privileged CSRs including mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie/mip, mstatus controls, satp/asid, dpc, dscratch0/1, cache-enable CSRs, performance CSRs / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software executing CSR operations Operation: read/write privileged CSR Object: privileged CSRs including mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie/mip, mstatus controls, satp/asid, dpc, dscratch0/1, cache-enable CSRs, performance CSRs Expected guard: CSR reads/writes should be mediated by current privilege/mode checks, CSR address privilege encoding, read/write access exceptions, and debug-mode-only rules for dpc/dscratch/dcsr. Observed behavior: Read logic directly returns DCSR/DPC/DSCRATCH, supervisor, and machine CSRs when csr_read selects the address. Write logic under csr_we directly updates DCSR and later machine CSRs such as MSTATUS/MEDELEG/MIDELEG/MIE/MTVEC. The provided search found no occurrences of csr_addr.priv/read_only/privilege access gating. assign priv_lvl_o = (debug_mode_q || umode_i) ? M : priv_lvl_q also elevates when umode_i is set. Illegal instruction generation is tied to update_access_exception/read_access_exception, but observed decode generally does not set these for privilege violations. Path: software CSR instruction path -> csr_regfile csr_addr_i/csr_op_i -> csr_read_process/csr_update_process -> protected CSR state and outputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If software in lower privilege can reach csr_regfile without an external decoder blocking it, it can read/write machine/debug/supervisor CSRs, alter trap/debug/interrupt/cache policy, or expose dpc/dscratch state.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Source snippets do not show typedef of csr_addr or any external instruction-decode privilege filtering; if gating exists outside csr_regfile it was not provided.

findings.summary: Virtual-memory and memory-permission policy CSRs are updated without visible privilege/TVM/TW/TSR enforcement in the observed csr_regfile decode.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure memory translation/access policy by writing SATP/MSTATUS and mode bits or executing returns / satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, SUM, MXR, MPRV, TVM/TW/TSR derived policy state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR requester Operation: configure memory translation/access policy by writing SATP/MSTATUS and mode bits or executing returns Object: satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, SUM, MXR, MPRV, TVM/TW/TSR derived policy state Expected guard: Only appropriately privileged software should modify translation/access policy controls; supervisor actions restricted by TVM/TW/TSR should be blocked when those bits are set. Observed behavior: SATP write converts csr_wdata to satp, masks ASID width, and updates satp_d if mode is OFF or SV39. MSTATUS write assigns mstatus_d=csr_wdata with masks. No observed checks of current privilege, TVM, or supervisor restrictions surround these updates. CSR operation logic asserts csr_we for write/set/clear irrespective of privilege; exception logic only reports update_access_exception/read_access_exception when set elsewhere. Path: csr_regfile csr_op_i/csr_addr_i -> SATP/MSTATUS write cases -> satp_q/mstatus_q/en_ld_st_translation_q -> MMU permission outputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower-privilege or unintended software could enable/disable translation, change ASID/PPN, or alter SUM/MXR/MPRV-like memory-access policy, potentially bypassing memory isolation or redirecting address translation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact output assignments for en_translation_o, SUM/MXR/MPRV/TVM/TW/TSR were truncated; current privilege representation beyond snippets is incomplete.

findings.summary: JTAG/AES key material is stored as reset-loaded ROM2 secure_reg but is writable through bus-facing rom2.we_i, readable through rdata_o, and used to populate jtag_key/pass without visible privilege/lock protection.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure/read/expose debug authentication key material or use key for pass check / jtag_key, ROM2 secure_reg key entries, pkey/key-location related data controlling debug authentication / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/MMIO requester and external JTAG requester Operation: configure/read/expose debug authentication key material or use key for pass check Object: jtag_key, ROM2 secure_reg key entries, pkey/key-location related data controlling debug authentication Expected guard: JTAG authentication key or key-location/configuration registers should be protected from unauthorized writes/reads and reset defaults should not enable debug access by default. Observed behavior: rom2 initializes const mem with fixed key values including a JTAG entry and AES entry. secure_reg is reset-loaded from mem, but on req_i && we_i writes secure_reg[index] <= wdata_i; rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q]. ariane_peripherals exposes rom2_req/rom2_we/rom2_addr/rom2_wdata from axi2mem and has jtag_key output/key_reg_out signals. dmi_jtag samples jtag_key into pass on reset. No visible lock, privilege, read masking, or lifecycle guard protects ROM2 key storage. Path: AXI/peripheral bus -> ariane_peripherals axi2rom2 -> rom2 req/we/addr/wdata -> secure_reg/rdata/key_reg_out/jtag_key -> dmi_jtag pass/pass_chk Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Software or bus masters may overwrite or read key material, allowing debug authentication bypass or secret disclosure; fixed reset constants further reduce key secrecy.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact ariane_peripherals assignment from key_reg_out to jtag_key was not fully shown; fact layer confirms jtag_key output and pkey/key signals.

findings.summary: ROM2 is writable despite containing boot/key material and being named ROM/fuse; write enable from bus-facing axi2mem directly updates secure_reg without authorization or write protection.
findings.vulnerability_category: write or overwrite ROM/key/boot data, or read ROM2 data / ROM2 contents secure_reg and rdata_o/key-related material / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: SoC bus/APB/AXI requester Operation: write or overwrite ROM/key/boot data, or read ROM2 data Object: ROM2 contents secure_reg and rdata_o/key-related material Expected guard: ROM2/boot/key material should be read-only or write-protected; any programming write should require privileged lifecycle/debug/programming authorization. Observed behavior: rom2 has req_i, we_i, addr_i, wdata_i. On reset secure_reg loads constant mem; after reset any req_i with we_i writes secure_reg at addr index with wdata_i. rdata_o returns secure_reg. ariane_peripherals wires ROM2 through axi2mem with rom2_we/rom2_wdata/rom2_addr. No guard beyond req_i/we_i or bounds check before the write is visible. Path: AXI fabric/peripheral region -> ariane_peripherals i_axi2rom2 -> rom2_req/rom2_we/rom2_addr/rom2_wdata -> i_rom2 secure_reg/rdata_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A bus master can corrupt or replace key/boot/access-control data stored in ROM2, potentially enabling JTAG bypass, crypto key manipulation, or access-policy corruption.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete address decode and bus privilege policy for rom2_fuse was truncated, but rom2.sv itself enforces no write protection.

findings.summary: ariane_peripherals contains APB-to-reg bridge instances and AES/packet APB signals (aes_penable/aes_pwrite/aes_paddr/aes_psel/aes_pwdata/aes_prdata; pkt_*). Fact layer shows bridge instantiation locations, but target AES/packet register implementations and read/write muxes were not provided in the tool observations. No concrete secret read or unguarded write behavior can be confirmed from the visible evidence alone.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write AES/packet security peripheral registers / AES/key-related APB registers and packet/security configuration signals aes_* and pkt_* / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software/APB requester Operation: read/write AES/packet security peripheral registers Object: AES/key-related APB registers and packet/security configuration signals aes_* and pkt_* Expected guard: Security-sensitive peripheral register banks should require privilege/range/lock checks and should mask secret key/intermediate/plaintext state on APB reads unless permitted. Observed behavior: ariane_peripherals contains APB-to-reg bridge instances and AES/packet APB signals (aes_penable/aes_pwrite/aes_paddr/aes_psel/aes_pwdata/aes_prdata; pkt_*). Fact layer shows bridge instantiation locations, but target AES/packet register implementations and read/write muxes were not provided in the tool observations. No concrete secret read or unguarded write behavior can be confirmed from the visible evidence alone. Path: AXI/APB bridge in ariane_peripherals -> i_apb_to_reg_aes and i_apb_to_reg_pkt -> target peripheral register signals/modules Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Potential exposure or modification of AES keys/config/plaintext could compromise cryptographic confidentiality/integrity, but no specific RTL behavior proving that impact is available in the provided observations.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need APB decode around AES/pkt, target register implementation, read data muxes, lock/privilege checks, and whether key/intermediate registers are readable.

findings.summary: Visible facts show debug AXI master/slave connections, SPI/Ethernet AXI clock converters/slaves, and awprot/arprot-like signals. dm_top connects dm_sba to axi_m_req_o and axi_slave_connect_rev/axi2mem for debug slave. ariane_peripherals has AXI/APB/peripheral bridge instances. However, connect module implementations, complete address decode constants, security attribute handling, and range enforcement were not included in observations.
findings.vulnerability_category: route read/write transactions across fabric / SoC address map regions reachable via AXI master/slave connects, including debug module, CLINT, boot ROM, peripherals, SPI/Ethernet bridges / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI bus master such as CPU, debug SBA master, or peripheral/DMA-like requester Operation: route read/write transactions across fabric Object: SoC address map regions reachable via AXI master/slave connects, including debug module, CLINT, boot ROM, peripherals, SPI/Ethernet bridges Expected guard: Fabric should preserve requester identity/security attributes and enforce address ranges so debug/DMA/external-facing controllers cannot access protected memory/MMIO outside authorized regions. Observed behavior: Visible facts show debug AXI master/slave connections, SPI/Ethernet AXI clock converters/slaves, and awprot/arprot-like signals. dm_top connects dm_sba to axi_m_req_o and axi_slave_connect_rev/axi2mem for debug slave. ariane_peripherals has AXI/APB/peripheral bridge instances. However, connect module implementations, complete address decode constants, security attribute handling, and range enforcement were not included in observations. Path: debug master/slave and peripheral AXI connections in dm_top/ariane_testharness/ariane_peripherals -> AXI fabric/connect modules -> memory/MMIO slaves Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If address decode or requester/security attributes are absent, debug or peripheral masters could reach protected memory/MMIO; provided evidence is insufficient to prove or disprove this fabric-level impact.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Need ariane_soc_pkg address constants, AXI connect implementation/decode, awprot/arprot/user tie-offs or propagation, and any PMP/fabric policy enforcement.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
